summary:
A history of stroke or venous thromboembolism does not contraindicate copper IUD use since the device is nonhormonal and lacks associated thrombotic risk. The only established contraindication among the options is active or recurrent pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), due to increased risk of worsened infection post-placement.  

\boxed{B}

The only established contraindication among the options is active or recurrent pelvic inflammatory disease (PID), due to increased risk of worsened infection post-placement.  

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
